source_note: FY2026 HUD Multifamily Tax Subsidy Project (MTSP) income limits, read verbatim from HUD's official machine-readable release MTSP-Data-FY26.xlsx (downloaded from huduser.gov on 2026-07-19). VERIFICATION: the pack-frozen Boston-Cambridge-Quincy table in pack/data/mtsp_2026_boston_cambridge_quincy.csv was compared row-by-row against this same official file and matches EXACTLY on all 8 household sizes and on median family income (164600). The pack table is real HUD data, not synthetic. All regions in this file therefore come from one consistent source and may be displayed together. Boston is marked is_pack_frozen=true because it is the copy the challenge pack froze for scoring; its values are identical to the HUD release.
derivation: PUBLISHED, not computed. Values are copied directly from HUD's published Lim60_26p1..Lim60_26p8 columns. Separately verified: across all 4,764 county rows in the FY2026 release, the published 60% value equals round(1.2 x published 50% VLI) with zero exceptions, so HUD's published 60% column and the 50%x1.2 computation coincide. The pack's Boston 60% column is likewise exactly 1.2x its 50% column. There is therefore no methodology mismatch between the pack table and these regions. No value in this file was interpolated, estimated, or back-filled.
household_size_note: limits_60_percent keys are household sizes 1-8. HUD derives non-4-person limits from the 4-person figure at 70/80/90/100/108/116/124/132 percent, rounded to the nearest $50. Households above 8 persons are not published here and must not be extrapolated in the UI.

regions.display_name: Boston-Cambridge-Quincy, MA-NH HUD Metro FMR Area
New York, NY HUD Metro FMR Area
Los Angeles-Long Beach-Glendale, CA HUD Metro FMR Area
Chicago-Joliet-Naperville, IL HUD Metro FMR Area
regions.hera_special_note: HUD publishes a separate, higher 'HERA Special' table for this area. It applies ONLY to projects placed in service in 2007 or 2008. limits_60_percent above is the standard table that applies to all other projects.

regions.display_name: Houston-The Woodlands-Sugar Land, TX HUD Metro FMR Area
Atlanta-Sandy Springs-Roswell, GA HUD Metro FMR Area
regions.hera_special_note: HUD publishes a separate, higher 'HERA Special' table for this area. It applies ONLY to projects placed in service in 2007 or 2008. limits_60_percent above is the standard table that applies to all other projects.

not_included.reason: HUD's FY2026 MTSP briefing states the FY2026 limits cover all areas EXCEPT the U.S. Virgin Islands and Guam. Excluded to avoid mixing fiscal years.

not_included.region: San Francisco, CA HUD Metro FMR Area
not_included.reason: Verified and available (4-person 60% = 126060), but omitted to keep the list at 7. Not a data-quality exclusion - can be added from the same file.
Out of scope for this demo slice. The full FY2026 release covers 2,639 distinct HUD areas across 4,764 county rows and can be ingested wholesale from the same xlsx with no additional sourcing work.
